content.subtitle: Use
rows: '**Have** you **been** to the gallery?' he asked her. → He asked her **if** she **had been** to the gallery.

rows.type: questions beginning with have, do or be

examples.direct: '**Have** you **been** to the gallery?' he asked her.
examples.reported: He asked her **if** she **had been** to the gallery.

examples.direct: '**Do** you **want** a sweater?' my mum asked.
examples.reported: My mum asked **if** I **wanted** a sweater.

examples.direct: '**Are** you **making** a skirt?' I asked Anne.
examples.reported: I asked Anne **if** she **was making** a skirt.

examples.direct: '**Can** you paint?' Mary asked her friend.
examples.reported: Mary asked her friend **if** he **could** paint.

examples.direct: '**Will** you make me one?' I asked Terry.
examples.reported: I asked Terry **if** he **would** make me one.

examples.direct: '**Shall** I wear a jacket?' I asked Mum.
examples.reported: I asked Mum **if** I **should** wear a jacket.

examples.direct: '**May** I borrow your coat?' Mr Jones asked me.
examples.reported: Mr Jones asked me **if** he **might** borrow my coat.

rows.type: questions beginning with a question word

examples.direct: '**What** kind of shoes are in fashion now?' my mum asked me.
examples.reported: My mum asked me **what** kind of shoes **were** in fashion at that moment.

examples.direct: '**Who** did you see at the fashion show?' asked Ben.
examples.reported: Ben asked **who** I **had seen** at the fashion show.

examples.direct: '**Which** one do you want?' Sarah asked Liam.
'**When** will they finish the house?' I asked.
examples.reported: I asked **when** they **would** finish the house.

examples.direct: '**Why** did you say that?' my sister asked me.
examples.reported: My sister asked me **why** I **had said** that.

examples.direct: '**How** much did your hat cost?' Ed asked Carl.
examples.reported: Ed asked Carl **how** much his hat **had cost**.

examples.direct: 'Put your clothes in the drawer,' Mum said.
examples.reported: Mum **told** me **to put** my clothes in the drawer.

examples.direct: 'Don't wear the red one,' Alice said.
examples.reported: Alice **told** me **not to wear** the red one.

examples.direct: 'Will you make me one?' I asked Terry.
'Please don't move my pictures,' said Olga.
examples.reported: Olga **asked** me **not to move** her pictures.

content.subtitle: Helpful hint
items: In reported questions, we can use **whether** instead of **if**.
He asked her whether she had been to the gallery.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: Do not use question word order in reported questions.

items.correct: I asked when they would finish the house.

items.incorrect: I asked when would they finish the house.

sections.title: A: Reported yes/no questions
sections.instructions: Rewrite the direct questions as reported questions using if.

items.sentence: "Do you need help?" she asked me.
items.answer: She asked me if I needed help.

items.sentence: "Have they finished the project?" Tom asked.
items.answer: Tom asked if they had finished the project.

items.sentence: "Can you drive?" my uncle asked me.
items.answer: My uncle asked me if I could drive.

items.sentence: "Will she come tomorrow?" he asked.
items.answer: He asked if she would come the next day.

items.sentence: "Should I call the manager?" Ana asked.
items.answer: Ana asked if she should call the manager.

items.sentence: "Did you see Ben?" Mum asked me.
items.answer: Mum asked me if I had seen Ben.

sections.title: B: Reported wh- questions
sections.instructions: Complete each reported question with the correct form.

items.sentence: "Where do they live?" she asked. → She asked where they ...... .
"Why are you late?" Dad asked me. → Dad asked me why I ...... late.
"When will the class start?" he asked. → He asked when the class ...... .
"Who did you meet?" Liam asked. → Liam asked who I ...... .
"How much did it cost?" I asked. → I asked how much it ...... .
"Which one do you want?" Sara asked. → Sara asked which one I ...... .
"Close the window," the teacher said to me.
items.answer: The teacher told me to close the window.

items.sentence: "Don't touch that button," Dad said to us.
items.answer: Dad told us not to touch that button.

items.sentence: "Please wait for me," Carla said to Ben.
items.answer: Carla asked Ben to wait for her.

items.sentence: "Please don't be late," the coach said to the team.
items.answer: The coach asked the team not to be late.

items.sentence: "Send me the file," the manager said to Tom.
items.answer: The manager told Tom to send him the file.

items.sentence: "Please don't use my laptop," Nina said to me.
items.answer: Nina asked me not to use her laptop.

items.sentence: "Are you busy?" she asked me. She asked me ...... busy.
"Why did you leave early?" he asked. He asked ...... early.
"Please sit down," the doctor said. The doctor ...... down.
"Don't open the door," Mum said. Mum told us ...... the door.
"Can you help me?" she asked. She asked me if I ...... help her.
"When will they arrive?" he asked. He asked when they ...... .

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
"Do you like jazz?" he asked me. **IF**
He asked me ...... jazz.

"Where did you buy that jacket?" Anna asked. **WHERE**
Anna asked ...... that jacket.

"Please call me tonight," she said to him. **ASKED**
She ...... her that night.

"Don't run in the corridor," the teacher said. **TOLD**
The teacher ...... in the corridor.

"When are they leaving?" I asked. **WHEN**
I asked ...... leaving.

"Can I borrow your notes?" Mark asked me. **WHETHER**
Mark asked me ...... my notes.
